description: Manage CourseCraft course content in Airtable

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management via Airtable PAT delegation. Use to login, logout, test credentials, or check auth status. CourseCraft delegates auth to the airtable CLI under the hood.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure Airtable authentication. Delegates to 'airtable auth login'. Use --force/-F to clear existing credentials and re-authenticate.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored Airtable credentials. Delegates to 'airtable auth logout'.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft auth profiles` for Manage authentication profiles commands. Check nested actions in `usage.json` before executing anything.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft auth profiles create` for Create a new profile from .env.example template.. Check this command's arguments and options in `usage.json` before execution.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft auth profiles delete` for Delete a profile and its data.. Check this command's arguments and options in `usage.json` before execution.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft auth profiles get` for Get details for a specific profile.. Check this command's arguments and options in `usage.json` before execution.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft auth profiles list` for List all profiles and show which is the default.. Check this command's arguments and options in `usage.json` before execution.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

rename.help: Rename a profile, re-keying its secrets to the new profile name.

arguments.name: new
arguments.help: New profile name (must not already exist)

options.name: --keep-old
options.help: Build and activate the new profile but leave the old profile dir and old secrets intact

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Check if authentication is configured and valid without making an API call. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify credentials are valid by making a test API call. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft cache` for Manage CLI cache commands. Check nested actions in `usage.json` before executing anything.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use `coursecraft cache clear` for Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.. Check this command's arguments and options in `usage.json` before execution.

clips.usage_instructions: CRUD operations for clip records within a module. Supports single and batch creation via --json/--file. Use --module/-m to filter by module, or --course to filter by course. The 'show' command displays an ASCII tree of the clip with its demos and slides. Accepts M1C1/M2C3 shorthand patterns or record IDs.

create.help: Create clip record(s) in Airtable. Supports two modes: 1. Single clip mode: Use --name to create one clip 2. Batch mode: Use --json or --file to create multiple clips
create.usage_instructions: Create one or more clip records. Requires --module/-m with module record ID. Single mode: use --name/-n (required), --order/-o, --story/-s, --target-length/-t, --status. Batch mode: use --json (inline JSON array) or --file (JSON file path) for multiple clips.

options.name: --json
options.help: Inline JSON array of clips (batch mode)

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to JSON file with clip definitions

delete.help: Delete a clip record. By default, only the clip record is deleted. Use --cascade to also delete all child records (demos, slides).
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a clip by record ID (positional argument). Use --cascade to also delete all child records (demos, slides). Use --force/-f to skip confirmation prompts.

get.help: Get a single clip record by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single clip by record ID (positional argument). Use --properties/-p to select specific JSON fields and --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

list.usage_instructions: List clip records. Use --module/-m to filter by module record ID. Use --filter/-f with field:op:value format (e.g., 'status:eq:Complete'). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --properties/-p for field selection. Use --limit/-l to cap results.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

show.help: Display clip hierarchy as an ASCII tree diagram. Shows the clip with all its demos and slides along with their statuses.
show.usage_instructions: Display a clip's full hierarchy as an ASCII tree. Accepts a record ID, M1C1/M2C3 shorthand pattern, or clip name (positional argument). Use --course/-c to scope the search when using shorthand patterns.

arguments.name: clip_identifier
arguments.help: Clip record ID, ID pattern (M1C1, M1C2), or name

options.name: --course
options.help: Course to scope the search (record ID or slug)

examples: coursecraft clips update recXXX --learning-objectives "- Objective 1\n- Objective 2"
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing clip by record ID (positional argument). All fields are optional: --name/-n, --order/-o, --story/-s, --learning-objectives/-l, --target-length, --status. Supports --brainstorming-outline/-b for outline content. The Clips table has no fact-check field; brainstorming-outline fact-check state lives on Courses, not Clips.

options.name: --content-done
options.help: Set or clear the clip content-structure-complete flag

options.name: --feedback-requested
options.help: Set or clear the feedback-requested gate flag

options.name: --feedback-requested-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback gate was requested

read.help: Read course outline data from a Google Doc.

options.name: --course-outline-link
options.help: Google Doc URL or document ID to read

update.help: Update course outline in Google Doc and/or Airtable database.

arguments.name: course
arguments.help: Course record ID or Course ID slug

options.name: --type
options.help: Update target(s): google_doc, database, or google_doc,database

options.name: --course-outline-file
options.help: Path to file (markdown for parsing or content)

options.name: --course-outline-link
options.help: Google Doc URL (only for database type - parses doc to markdown)

courses.usage_instructions: CRUD operations for course records. Supports nested creation of modules and clips via --json/--file. Use --active/-a on list to get only the active course. Use --include-modules/-m and --include-clips/-c on get to embed child records. Courses are identified by record ID or Course ID slug.

create.help: Create a course record, optionally with modules and clips.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new course record. Requires --name/-n, --course-id/-c (slug), and --target-length/-t. Supports nested creation of modules and clips via --json (inline JSON) or --file (JSON file path). Optional fields include --platform, --short-description, --long-description, --content-level, --job-role, --learner-profile, --prerequisites, --storyline, --learning-objectives, --notes, and --active.

options.name: --course-outline-link
options.help: Google Doc URL for course outline

options.name: --recording-dictation-method
options.help: [Automatic Narration Generation|Manual Instructor Generation]  Recording dictation method (Automatic Narration Generation, Manual Instructor Generation)

options.name: --json
options.help: Inline JSON for nested module/clip creation

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to JSON file with module/clip definitions

delete.help: Delete a course record. By default, only the course record is deleted. Use --cascade to also delete all child records (modules, clips, demos, slides).
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a course record. Requires --course/-c with record ID or slug. Use --cascade to also delete all child records (modules, clips, demos, slides). Use --force/-f to skip confirmation prompts.

options.name: --course
options.help: Course record ID or Course ID slug

options.name: --cascade
options.help: Delete all child records (modules, clips, demos, slides)

get.help: Get a single course record by ID or slug. Use --include-modules to embed module records in the JSON output. Use --include-clips to also embed clip records within each module.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single course by record ID or Course ID slug (positional argument). Use --properties/-p to select specific JSON fields. Use --include-modules/-m to embed module records. Use --include-clips/-c to embed clip records within each module (implies --include-modules). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: course
arguments.help: Course record ID or Course ID slug

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

options.name: --include-modules
options.help: Include nested module records in output

list.usage_instructions: List all course records. Use --active/-a to return only the active course. Use --filter/-f with field:op:value format for filtering (e.g., 'active:eq:true'). Use --table/-t for formatted table output. Use --properties/-p for field selection with comma-separated dot notation. Use --limit/-l to cap the number of results.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

update.usage_instructions: Update an existing course by record ID or slug (positional argument). All fields are optional -- only provided fields are updated. Key fields: --name/-n, --course-id/-c, --target-length/-t, --status/-s, --active, --platform, --short-description, --long-description, --content-level, --content-tags, --job-role, --learner-profile, --prerequisites, --platform-versions, --storyline, --learning-objectives, --notes, --skill-path, --path-placement. Supports --brainstorming-outline/-B or --brainstorming-outline-file for outline content, and --brainstorming-outline-fact-checked to mark as reviewed. Supports --course-outline or --course-outline-file for outline content.

arguments.name: course
arguments.help: Course record ID or Course ID slug

options.name: --course-outline-link
options.help: Google Doc URL for course outline

options.name: --course-outline-file
options.help: Path to file containing course outline content

options.name: --course-outline-submitted
options.help: Mark whether the course outline has been submitted to Pluralsight

options.name: --course-outline-approved-pluralsight
options.help: Mark whether the course outline has been approved by Pluralsight

options.name: --research-report-file
options.help: Path to file containing research report content

options.name: --slack-channel-name
options.help: Slack channel name for Pluralsight course communication

options.name: --recording-dictation-method
options.help: [Automatic Narration Generation|Manual Instructor      Recording dictation method (Automatic Narration Generation]                                            Generation, Manual Instructor Generation)

options.name: --brainstorming-outline-file
options.help: Path to file containing brainstorming outline

options.name: --brainstorming-outline-fact-checked
options.help: Set or clear the brainstorming outline fact-checked flag

options.name: --feedback-requested
options.help: Set or clear the feedback-requested gate flag

options.name: --feedback-requested-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback gate was requested

demos.usage_instructions: CRUD operations for demo records linked to clips. Supports hierarchical filtering: --clip/-c for a specific clip, --module/-m for all demos in a module, and --course for all demos in a course. Demo fields include demo-walkthrough-script-path, demo-walkthrough-script-created, action-summary, script, and recap. Use --clip-order/-o to set position within the clip (shared ordering with slides).

create.help: Create demo record(s) in Airtable linked to a clip. Supports two modes: 1. Single demo mode: Use optional fields to create one demo 2. Batch mode: Use --json or --file to create multiple demos The --clip-order is required and specifies the position of the demo within the clip, alongside slides which also have a clip order.
create.usage_instructions: Create one or more demo records. Requires --clip/-c (clip record ID) and --clip-order/-o (position within clip). Single mode: optional --name/-n, --target-length/-l (FLOAT, e.g., 2.5), --action-summary/-a, --script/-s. Batch mode: use --json (inline JSON array) or --file (JSON file path). Demo environment facts are owned by the project-level demo-environment skill, not linked Airtable records.

options.name: --demo-overview
options.help: High-level instructional overview for the demo

options.name: --execution-method
options.help: [Automated Walkthrough|Manual Instructor]  Demo execution method: 'Automated Walkthrough' (agent builds the walkthrough) or 'Manual Instructor' (instructor records live; no walkthrough)

options.name: --json
options.help: Inline JSON array of demos (batch mode)

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to JSON file with demo definitions

delete.help: Delete a demo record. This action is PERMANENT and cannot be undone.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a demo by record ID (positional argument). This is permanent. Use --force/-f to skip confirmation prompts.

get.help: Get a single demo record by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single demo by record ID (positional argument). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

list.usage_instructions: List demo records with hierarchical filtering. Use --clip/-c for a specific clip, --module/-m for all demos in a module, or --course for all demos in a course. Use --filter/-f with field:op:value format (e.g., 'status:eq:Complete', 'fields.Name:contains:Setup'). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --properties/-p for field selection. Use --limit/-l to cap results.

options.name: --module
options.help: Filter by module record ID (gets all demos in module)

options.name: --course
options.help: Filter by course slug or record ID (gets all demos in course)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

examples: coursecraft demos update recXXX --action-summary "Step-by-step demo flow..."
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing demo by record ID (positional argument). Key content fields: --name/-n, --clip-order/-o, --target-length/-l (FLOAT), --action-summary/-a, --script/-s, --recap, --demo-walkthrough-script-path, and --demo-walkthrough-script-created. Review fields: --action-summary-review-ai, --action-summary-human-review-complete, --script-review-human. Status fields: --tested-approved, --dictation-recorded, and --recorded. Demo environment facts are owned by the project-level demo-environment skill, not linked Airtable records.

options.name: --clip
options.help: Re-parent the demo to this clip record ID

options.name: --demo-overview
options.help: High-level instructional overview for the demo

options.name: --demo-overview-review-human
options.help: Mark demo overview human review as complete

options.name: --execution-method
options.help: [Automated Walkthrough|Manual Instructor]  Demo execution method: 'Automated Walkthrough' (agent builds the walkthrough) or 'Manual Instructor' (instructor records live; no walkthrough)

options.name: --action-summary-review-ai
options.help: AI review of the action summary

options.name: --action-summary-review-human
options.help: Mark action summary human review as complete

options.name: --tested-approved
options.help: Mark demo as tested and approved

options.name: --script-review-human
options.help: Mark script human review as complete

options.name: --demo-walkthrough-script-created
options.help: Mark whether the demo walkthrough automation has been created for this demo

options.name: --dictation-recorded
options.help: Mark demo dictation audio as recorded

options.name: --voice-recording-path
options.help: Path to the instructor-recorded voice audio for this demo

options.name: --audio-synced
options.help: Mark demo narration audio as synced onto the recorded video

options.name: --feedback-requested
options.help: Set or clear the feedback-requested gate flag

options.name: --feedback-requested-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback gate was requested

descript.usage_instructions: Export video clips from Descript projects to course clip folders on Google Drive. Requires Descript to be running with the project open. Finds the project by name (partial match) and locates the composition matching the module/clip pattern.

export.help: Export a Descript composition to the course clips folder. Finds the Descript project by name, locates the composition matching the module/clip pattern (e.g., "m2c1"), and exports the full composition (with slides, edits, etc.) via the Descript app's local export. Requires Descript to be running with the project open.
examples: coursecraft descript export "Advanced Features of Cursor AI" -m 2 -c 1
coursecraft descript export "Advanced Features of Cursor AI" -m 2 -c 1 --course advanced-features-cursor-ai
coursecraft descript export "Advanced Features of Cursor AI" -m 2 -c 1 --dry-run
export.usage_instructions: Export a Descript composition to the course clips folder. Positional argument is the project name (partial match supported). Requires --module/-m (module number) and --clip/-c (clip number). Optional --course to override active course slug. Use --dry-run/-n to preview the export without executing.

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Show what would be done without exporting

options.name: --timestamp
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp; defaults to the current UTC time when omitted

options.name: --processing-status
options.help: Processing status (Pending, Proposed, Applied, Declined, Failed, No Action)

options.name: --processed-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback was processed

options.name: --remediation
options.help: Summary of which skills/agents/sources were modified and how, to remediate this feedback (orchestrator-written when set to Applied)

options.name: --attribute-name
options.help: The artifact field the feedback is about, e.g. Script

options.name: --attribute-snapshot
options.help: The entire attribute value frozen at submission time

options.name: --selected-text
options.help: The exact span the comment targets

get.help: Get a single feedback record by ID.

options.name: --demo
options.help: Filter by linked demo record ID (client-side)

options.name: --slide
options.help: Filter by linked slide record ID (client-side)

options.name: --clip
options.help: Filter by linked clip record ID (client-side)

options.name: --module
options.help: Filter by linked module record ID (client-side)

options.name: --course
options.help: Filter by linked course record ID (client-side)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

options.name: --processing-status
options.help: Processing status (Pending, Proposed, Applied, Declined, Failed, No Action)

options.name: --processed-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback was processed

options.name: --remediation
options.help: Summary of which skills/agents/sources were modified and how, to remediate this feedback (orchestrator-written when set to Applied)

options.name: --attribute-name
options.help: The artifact field the feedback is about, e.g. Script

options.name: --attribute-snapshot
options.help: The entire attribute value frozen at submission time

options.name: --selected-text
options.help: The exact span the comment targets

modules.usage_instructions: CRUD operations for module records within a course. Supports batch clip creation via --json/--file on create. Use --course/-c to filter by course slug or record ID. The 'show' command displays an ASCII tree of the module hierarchy with statuses. Accepts M1/M2 shorthand patterns or record IDs.

create.help: Create a module record, optionally with clips.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new module record. Requires --name/-n and --course/-c (record ID or slug). Optional: --order/-o, --description/-d, --learning-objectives/-l, --target-length/-t, --status/-s. Supports batch clip creation via --json (inline JSON array) or --file (JSON file path).

options.name: --course
options.help: Course record ID or Course ID (slug)

options.name: --json
options.help: Inline JSON array of clips to create

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to JSON file with clip definitions

delete.help: Delete a module record. By default, only the module record is deleted. Use --cascade to also delete all child records (clips, demos, slides).
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a module by record ID (positional argument). Use --cascade to also delete all child records (clips, demos, slides). Use --force/-f to skip confirmation prompts.

options.name: --cascade
options.help: Delete all child records (clips, demos, slides)

get.help: Get a single module record by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single module by record ID (positional argument). Use --properties/-p to select specific JSON fields. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

list.usage_instructions: List module records. Use --course/-c to filter by course record ID or slug. Use --filter/-f with field:op:value format (e.g., 'status:eq:Complete'). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --properties/-p for field selection. Use --limit/-l to cap results.

options.name: --course
options.help: Filter by course record ID or Course ID slug

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

show.help: Display module hierarchy as an ASCII tree diagram. Shows the module with all its clips, demos, and slides along with their statuses.
show.usage_instructions: Display a module's full hierarchy as an ASCII tree. Accepts a record ID, M1/M2 shorthand pattern, or module name (positional argument). Use --course/-c to scope the search when using shorthand patterns.

arguments.name: module_identifier
arguments.help: Module record ID, ID pattern (M1, M2), or name

options.name: --course
options.help: Course to scope the search (record ID or slug)

update.usage_instructions: Update an existing module by record ID (positional argument). All fields are optional: --name/-n, --order/-o, --description/-d, --learning-objectives/-l, --target-length, --status/-s. Supports --brainstorming-outline/-b or --brainstorming-outline-file for outline content. The Modules table has no fact-check field; brainstorming-outline fact-check state lives on Courses, not Modules. Use --slides-submitted for Pluralsight slide deck submission tracking.

options.name: --brainstorming-outline-file
options.help: Path to file containing brainstorming outline

options.name: --module-review-complete
options.help: Set or clear the module review complete flag

options.name: --slides-submitted
options.help: Set or clear the slide deck submitted-for-review flag (Pluralsight only)

options.name: --pluralsight-slides-approved
options.help: Set or clear the Pluralsight slides-approved flag, the gate trigger that releases 'Waiting for Slide Approval' to recording (Pluralsight only)

options.name: --slide-narration-approved
options.help: Set or clear the slide-narration-approved flag, the gate that releases the slide-narration phase (Review Slide Narration) to recording

options.name: --slide-narration-recorded
options.help: Set or clear the slide-narration-recorded flag, marking the module's slide narration take as recorded

options.name: --feedback-requested
options.help: Set or clear the feedback-requested gate flag

options.name: --feedback-requested-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback gate was requested

slide-templates.usage_instructions: Manage PowerPoint slide template definitions stored in Airtable. Templates define the available slide layouts (e.g., Course Title, Remember This, Demo Intro). Use list with --platform/-P to filter by platform (Pluralsight, Udemy). Templates are referenced by record ID or name when creating/updating slides.

create.usage_instructions: Create a new slide template. Requires --name/-n. Optional: --platform/-P (Pluralsight, Udemy), --description/-d, --use-cases/-u, --deck-number (slide number in the template deck), --animations/-a.

options.name: --template-deck-version
options.help: Template deck version (e.g., '2026.05.a'); stored verbatim in the singleSelect field

options.name: --placeholders
options.help: Placeholders JSON blob (string). --placeholders-file wins if both given

options.name: --placeholders-file
options.help: Path to a file containing the Placeholders JSON blob

options.name: --image
options.help: Local image file to upload to the Image attachment field

options.name: --image-url
options.help: URL to attach to the Image field (alternative to --image)

get.help: Get a single slide template by ID or name.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single slide template by record ID or name (positional argument). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.usage_instructions: List slide template records. Use --platform/-P to filter by platform (e.g., 'Pluralsight', 'Udemy'). Use --filter/-f with field:op:value format (e.g., 'name:contains:Title'). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --properties/-p for field selection. Use --limit/-l to cap results.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

examples: coursecraft templates update recXXX --use-cases "New use cases"
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing slide template by record ID (positional argument). All fields are optional: --name/-n, --platform/-P, --description/-d, --use-cases/-u, --deck-number, --animations/-a.

options.name: --template-deck-version
options.help: Template deck version (e.g., '2026.05.a'); stored verbatim in the singleSelect field

options.name: --placeholders
options.help: Placeholders JSON blob (string). --placeholders-file wins if both given

options.name: --placeholders-file
options.help: Path to a file containing the Placeholders JSON blob

options.name: --image
options.help: Local image file to upload to the Image attachment field

options.name: --image-url
options.help: URL to attach to the Image field (alternative to --image)

slides.usage_instructions: CRUD operations for slide records linked to clips. Supports hierarchical filtering: --clip/-c for a specific clip, --module/-m for all slides in a module, --course for all slides in a course. Slide fields include build-instructions, script, template (record ID), clip-order, and demo (record ID for demo intro slides). Use --template/-t on create to set the slide template.

create.help: Create slide record(s) in Airtable linked to a clip. Supports two modes: 1. Single slide mode: Use --template to create one slide 2. Batch mode: Use --json or --file to create multiple slides
create.usage_instructions: Create one or more slide records. Requires --clip/-c (clip record ID). Single mode: use --template/-t (template record ID), --target-length/-l (FLOAT, e.g., 0.5). Batch mode: use --json (inline JSON array) or --file (JSON file path).

options.name: --json
options.help: Inline JSON array of slides (batch mode)

options.name: --file
options.help: Path to JSON file with slide definitions

delete.help: Delete a slide record. This action is PERMANENT and cannot be undone.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a slide by record ID (positional argument). This is permanent. Use --force/-f to skip confirmation prompts.

get.help: Get a single slide record by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a single slide by record ID (positional argument). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.usage_instructions: List slide records with hierarchical filtering. Use --clip/-c for a specific clip, --module/-m for all slides in a module, or --course for all slides in a course. Use --filter/-f with field:op:value format (e.g., 'name:contains:intro'). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --properties/-p for field selection. Use --limit/-l to cap results.

options.name: --module
options.help: Filter by module record ID (gets all slides in module)

options.name: --course
options.help: Filter by course slug or record ID (gets all slides in course)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

examples: coursecraft slides update recXXX --name "Demo Intro: Setup Demo"
coursecraft slides update recXXX --script "In this demo, we will..."
coursecraft slides update recXXX --build-instructions "Configure the API connection"
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing slide by record ID (positional argument). Key fields: --name/-n, --template/-t (template record ID), --clip-order/-o, --target-length/-l (FLOAT), --script/-s, --build-instructions/-b, --status, --demo/-d (demo record ID for demo intro slides).

options.name: --clip
options.help: Parent clip record ID (re-parent slide to a different clip)

options.name: --dictation-recorded
options.help: Mark slide dictation audio as recorded

options.name: --voice-recording-path
options.help: Path to the instructor-recorded voice audio for this slide

options.name: --demo
options.help: Demo record ID (for demo intro slides)

options.name: --feedback-requested
options.help: Set or clear the feedback-requested gate flag

options.name: --feedback-requested-at
options.help: ISO 8601 timestamp the feedback gate was requested

voice-recordings.help: Generate slide and demo voice recordings
voice-recordings.usage_instructions: Generate narration audio for one slide or demo record using the ElevenLabs CLI. Requires exactly one of --slide or --demo, plus explicit --voice-id, --output-format, and --output-dir. Defaults to model eleven_multilingual_v2 because CourseCraft narration uses a Professional Voice Clone and Eleven v3 does not currently support PVCs; pass --model-id only when a different compatible model is required. The command sanitizes non-spoken script cues, applies packaged regex pronunciation transforms for dynamic code-shaped text, syncs packaged alias rules to the ElevenLabs pronunciation dictionary named CourseCraft Voice Pronunciations, passes that dictionary locator to speech generation, writes an MP3 under slides/ or demos/ inside the output directory, validates the file, stores recording metadata on the CourseCraft record, and marks Dictation Recorded.

generate.help: Generate a voice recording for one slide or demo script.
generate.usage_instructions: Generate a voice recording from the Script field on one slide or demo record. Provide exactly one record option: --slide recXXX or --demo recXXX. Provide the ElevenLabs voice ID, output format, and output directory explicitly; --model-id defaults to eleven_multilingual_v2 because CourseCraft narration uses a Professional Voice Clone and Eleven v3 does not currently support PVCs. The command strips recording cues, applies regex transforms from coursecraft_cli/voice_pronunciation_patterns.json and coursecraft_cli/voice_pronunciation_tokens.json, syncs alias rules from coursecraft_cli/voice_pronunciations.json to the ElevenLabs dictionary named CourseCraft Voice Pronunciations, sends the normalized text to ElevenLabs with that dictionary locator, and records the normalized source hash.

options.name: --model-id
options.help: ElevenLabs model ID. Defaults to eleven_multilingual_v2 because Eleven v3 does not currently support PVCs

usage_instructions: Use for managing CourseCraft course content stored in Airtable. Manages a hierarchy of courses, modules, clips, demos, and slides. Commands follow the `coursecraft <group> <action>` pattern. Most list commands support --table/-t for formatted output, --filter/-f for field:op:value filtering, --properties/-p for field selection, and --limit/-l for pagination. Use --no-cache globally to bypass response cache. Use voice-recordings generate for optional recording-phase narration audio generation with ElevenLabs.
